description_long: This object is a large, mounted automatic weapon system, likely a heavy machine gun or autocannon, designed for stationary use. The main body is cylindrical and robust, with a reinforced base and a prominent, elongated barrel extending from the front. Attached to the side is a dome-shaped sensor or targeting module, featuring a pair of circular lenses or optics. The structure includes various mechanical components such as mounting brackets, support arms, and access panels, all rendered in a uniform matte gray finish. The weapon appears to be constructed primarily from metal, with some possible composite or polymer elements in the sensor housing. Its size and complexity suggest it is intended for vehicle or fixed emplacement use in urban defense or security scenarios.
description: A large, mounted automatic weapon system with a long barrel, cylindrical base, and a dome-shaped sensor module, all in matte gray metal. Designed for stationary or vehicle-mounted use in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barrel pointing forward, the cylindrical base, and the dome-shaped sensor module with visible optics on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the barrel, the angular body, and the sensor module positioned above the main housing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the mounting structure, open access area, and the back of the sensor module.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the barrel extending to the right and the sensor module on the far side, with the base and mounting details visible.
